column1: # 30 English classics + 30 Icelandic pairs built from common IS typo
# patterns: missing accents, adjacent-key hits on the Icelandic layout,
# dropped/doubled letters, and d/ð, o/ö, þ/ð orthographic confusions.
# split: space-miss pairs — pure insertion splits and space-substitution
# splits (c v b n m consumed as the missed space), incl. half repairs.
# dogfood wave (2026-07-15) — NEW categories, reported separately from the
# gating categories above (en/is/split must not regress; these are the
# under-firing repairs from the real dogfood line plus their class-mates).
# acc1: single-letter accent restoration ("giskar a allt" → á). Neutral
# posterior in this harness ⇒ offered, never auto-applied (lane-gated).
# drop: single dropped letter ("aveg" = alveg minus the l)
# (not "fallegr": that is one ADJACENT-KEY substitution from "fallegt",
# which is the better spatial reading — a fine top-1, just not this class)
# gemacc: gemination + accent compositions ("nogg" = nóg with doubled g and
# hard: accent-drop in the prefix + omission in the tail (the 4b
# diacritic-restored prefix-completion pass; dogfood "fara lega" token)
# spatial2: multi-position adjacent-key noise on the Icelandic layout — the
# beam-decoder class (dogfood "koetip" = kortið with e→r + p→ð). Two
# adjacent-key substitutions per word, plus a couple of triple-noise cases;
# unreachable by edits1/targeted passes, decoded by the multi-edit beam.
# Lane relaxation wave (2026-07-16) — PLAN.md "Lane relaxation profiles".
# accentlazy: accent-naked Icelandic (only the six long-press acutes áéíóúý
# stripped; ð þ æ ö have dedicated keys and never fold), judged inside a
# SATURATED IS lane (posterior primed to the 0.9 ceiling). Rows GENERATED
# from data/eval/sentences.is.txt, held-out-style: sentences from line 6000
# on, first qualifying word per sentence (>= 1 acute, skeleton length >= 4,
# corpus count >= 8), previous corpus word as 4th-column context. The
# generator also emits the AccentWordlists Swift literals (unigram = corpus
# count / 8, context bigram = count / 40) — see Wordlists.swift; these
# categories run on the SEPARATE accent engine so the categories above stay
# skeleton-collision positives: restoration may pass the valid-word rule
# only through the triple gate (+ sletta guard); "for" is EN-valid, fór
# dominates with "ég" context; lone i restores via the single-letter path.
# accentguard: collision skeletons in skeleton-meaning contexts — typo ==
# expected means the row PASSES only when nothing auto-applies (any firing
# = false restoration; the metric that matters). víst/vist ratio 7.6 fails
# dominance; "í vist" additionally fails the context gate; ver >> vér and
# van (English) / ván (rare) exercise the wrong-way and sletta guards.
# apos: the English mirror — apostrophe folding at a saturated EN lane
# (dont→don't at ~ε) + lone i→I capitalization. Hand-authored contraction
# pack with realistic relative counts (see AccentWordlists docs).
# aposguard: attested English skeletons keep their reading — cant (140) vs
# can't (150) and ill (90) vs i'll (55) fail the dominance ratio; well is
